lore.oath: Serve from birth to death. Every Wolf is born into the regiment. There are no civilians in the Pack.
lore.tone: Disciplined, cultured soldiers who love the hunt, the opera, and the law.
lore.relationship: Protect the Nobility, but do not obey them blindly. A General may grant a Duke a favor, but the Duke cannot order the General to march.

religion.failure: Wolf folklore states that eons ago, the Wolves were the original Guardians of the Sky. They failed in their duty, and as punishment, the Moon was shattered.
religion.penance: They swore a holy vow to protect the 'Earth-Bound' (the weak) so that such a catastrophe never happens again. They view their service as religious atonement.
religion.guidance: On the full moon or new moon or half waned, halved waxed, etc. depending on the exact situation is, a complex formula of subject, goal, current status, sex, age, and rank, even the last meal you had, or if you ever stepped on a crawfish before, etc. All factors into when you should howl or whimper at the temple, in triumph or penance.

clergy.alpha_priest: Called whelps until again the moon blesses them and they are anointed priests. Alpha is decided by fatal one on one combat under a full moon with ceremonial moon daggers.

body.armed_combat: Chosen through physical combat tournaments. 1. Armed combat until first blood and yield. 2 of three with each round being one combatant's weapon choice and the third if needed chosen by [[Valen the Witness]]
body.stalking: A stalking type challenge, 4 of 7 winner is successful doing the stalking and survives being stalked twice each to win. Can take days sometimes to complete.
body.unarmed: Unarmed one on one till submission or unconsciousness.

mind.riddle: A 4 out of 7 riddle contest, quite intense as an hour is given to answer for each riddle.
mind.moonstones: A 2 out of 3 match of Moonstones (chess like chinese checkers hybrid with multi colored stones and strategic board positions).
mind.debate: A debate and judgement challenge, grievances are presented and each makes judgement and then they argue against the other's case with literary quotes, poetry recitals, anecdotes and rebuttals and objections. Until one concedes twice.

selection.prime_alpha: To hold the supreme seat, one must defeat all 6 other Alphas in their respective fields (3 in combat, 3 in wits). This can be over time and can still count a win even if they have lost to that alpha since the win as long as the prime alpha hasn't changed.

challenge.rank: Rank is fluid. At any time, an Alpha can challenge another to switch seats.

moon_blessed.goal: To form a 'Perfect Pack' of 12 Moon-Blessed Wolves, containing one of each magical affinity.
moon_blessed.role: A Perfect Pack acts as the autonomous agents of an Alpha—spies, investigators, and secret police.
moon_blessed.shame: Ideally, there are 7 Perfect Packs (one for each Alpha). In reality, they are often short a few members. These 'Half-Packs' are terrified of insulting the Moon with their incompleteness, so they only perform minor tasks until they find their missing members.

culture.poets: Wolves are not just soldiers; they are Warrior-Poets.

rotation.week: Packs get one week off twice a year.
rotation.month: Every 5 years, a Pack gets a full month off.

culture.festival: When a Pack is on its 'Month Off,' they don't rest. They host a massive Tournament and Festival for the other Packs currently on their 'Week Off.'

coyotes.role: Because they are smaller, they handle Internal Security—town patrols, granary guards, and dock inspections.
coyotes.corruption: Coyotes are the ones dealing with the Otters (Smugglers) and Rats (Bankers). They often take bribes.
coyotes.legend: Only one Coyote has ever cracked the 'Alpha Seven.' His name is lost to history, but stories say he won not by strength, but by a trick so clever the Prime Alpha stepped down laughing.

rogues.exodus: Some Wolves are born too wild, too solitary, or simply disagree with the strict 'Moon Cult.' They leave the service young.
rogues.life: They wander the world seeking a 'Pseudo-Pack.' You find these Rogue Wolves as: Airship Marines, Mercenaries, or Circus Performers.
rogues.stigma: To a loyal Marshal, a Rogue is tragic—a soul wandering without the Moon's guidance.
